title: senna macranthera var. andina
intro: Senna macranthera var. andina is a perennial plant species belonging to the legume family, native to the Andean region of South America. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly as a laxative due to its high content of anthraquinone glycosides, which stimulate bowel movements and relieve constipation. The plant has various health benefits, including aiding in digestion, reducing inflammation, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. Senna macranthera var. andina is traditionally used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and capsules, which are consumed orally to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
